title: Programmed Harvard Mark I
description: Began her computing career as one of the first programmers on the Harvard Mark I electro-mechanical computer.

title: First Computer Bug
description: Popularized the term 'debugging' after finding a real moth trapped inside the relays of the Harvard Mark II computer.

title: Invented the Compiler
description: Developed the A-0 System, the world's first operational compiler, translating mathematical code into binary machine code.

title: Co-created COBOL
description: Played a key role in designing COBOL (Common Business-Oriented Language), a pioneer English-like business programming language.

title: Retired from Navy
description: Retired from the United States Navy as a Rear Admiral, being the oldest active-duty officer at the time of retirement.

title: Death
description: Hopper passed away in her sleep on January 1, 1992, and was buried with full military honors at Arlington National Cemetery.
